input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `Suspension`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `Suspension` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: v.3.1.4
            TITLE: fasteners in the suspension system are  critical fasteners, see t.8.2

            CONTENT:
            rule V.3.1.4: Fasteners in the Suspension system are  Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2
V.3.1.4 Fasteners in the Suspension system are  Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2

RULE: t.1.4.2
            TITLE: any mechanism in the throttle system that could become jammed must be covered.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.4.2: Any mechanism in the throttle system that could become jammed must be covered.
T.1.4.2 Any mechanism in the throttle system that could become jammed must be covered. 
This is to prevent debris or interference and includes but is not limited to a gear mechanism

RULE: v.3.2.4
            TITLE: the steering system must have positive steering stops that prevent the steering linkages from

            CONTENT:
            rule V.3.2.4: The steering system must have positive steering stops that prevent the steering linkages from
V.3.2.4 The steering system must have positive steering stops that prevent the steering linkages from 
locking up (the inversion of a four bar linkage at one of the pivots). The stops: 
a. Must prevent the wheels and tires from contacting suspension, bodywork, or Chassis 
during the track events 
b. May be placed on the uprights or on the rack

RULE: t.2.1
            TITLE: harness definitions

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.1: Harness Definitions
T.2.1 Harness Definitions 
a. 5 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts and one Anti-Submarine 
Belt.  
b. 6 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts and two leg or Anti-
Submarine Belts.  
c. 7 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts, two leg or Anti-
Submarine Belts and a negative g or Z Belt.   
d. Upright Driving Position - with a seat back angled at 30° or less from the vertical as 
measured along the line joining the two 200 mm circles of the template of the 95th 
percentile male as defined in F.5.6.4 and positioned per F.5.6.5 
e. Reclined Driving Position - with a seat back angled at more than 30° from the vertical as 
measured along the line joining the two 200 mm circles of the template of the 95th 
percentile male as defined in F.5.6.4 and positioned per F.5.6.5 
f. Chest to Groin Line - the straight line that in side view follows the line of the Shoulder 
Belts from the chest to the release buckle.

RULE: t.1.9.1
            TITLE: tractive system firewalls must meet the requirements of t.1.8 above

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.9.1: Tractive System Firewalls must meet the requirements of T.1.8 above
T.1.9.1 Tractive System Firewalls must meet the requirements of T.1.8 above

RULE: f.5.4.3
            TITLE: bolted connections in the primary structure using tabs or brackets must have an edge

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.4.3: Bolted connections in the Primary Structure using tabs or brackets must have an edge
F.5.4.3 Bolted connections in the Primary Structure using tabs or brackets must have an edge 
distance ratio “e/D” of 1.5 or higher  
“D” equals the hole diameter.  “e” equals the distance from the edge of the hole to the nearest 
free edge 
Tabs attaching the Suspension to the Primary Structure are NOT “in the Primary Structure”
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 32 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: d.12.9.2
            TITLE: if a vehicle stalls, or ingests a cone, etc., it may be allowed to continue, subject to d.12.1.4

            CONTENT:
            rule D.12.9.2: If a vehicle stalls, or ingests a cone, etc., it may be allowed to continue, subject to D.12.1.4
D.12.9.2 If a vehicle stalls, or ingests a cone, etc., it may be allowed to continue, subject to D.12.1.4 
and D.12.4

RULE: t.1.7.1
            TITLE: all vehicles must have a floor closeout to prevent track debris from entering

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.7.1: All vehicles must have a Floor Closeout to prevent track debris from entering
T.1.7.1 All vehicles must have a Floor Closeout to prevent track debris from entering

RULE: d.12.8.1
            TITLE: the driver change will be performed in this sequence:

            CONTENT:
            rule D.12.8.1: The Driver Change will be performed in this sequence:
D.12.8.1 The Driver Change will be performed in this sequence: 
a. Vehicle will stop in Driver Change Area
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 136 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
b. First Driver turns off the engine / Tractive System.  Driver Change time starts.   
c. First Driver exits the vehicle  
d. Any necessary adjustments may be made to the vehicle to fit the Second Driver IN.14.2.2 
e. Second Driver is secured in the vehicle 
f. Second Driver is ready to start the engine / enable the Tractive System.  Driver Change 
time stops.   
g. Second Driver receives permission to continue 
h. The vehicle engine is started or Tractive System enabled.  See D.12.4 
i. The vehicle stages to go back onto course, at the direction of the event officials

RULE: t.1.6.3
            TITLE: the design must address all three types of heat transfer between the heat source (examples

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.6.3: The design must address all three types of heat transfer between the heat source (examples
T.1.6.3 The design must address all three types of heat transfer between the heat source (examples 
include but are not limited to:  exhaust pipe, coolant hose/tube, Accumulator Container) and 
the panel that the driver could contact (seat or floor): 
a. Conduction Isolation by one of the following: 
• No direct contact between the heat source and the panel 
• A heat resistant, conduction isolation material with a minimum thickness of 8 mm 
between the heat source and the panel  
b. Convection Isolation by a minimum air gap of 25 mm between the heat source and the 
panel 
c. Radiation Isolation by one of the following: 
• A solid metal heat shield with a minimum thickness of 0.4 mm 
• Reflective foil or tape when combined with conduction insulation

RULE: t.1.4.4
            TITLE: all vehicle controls must stay below the top-most point of the front hoop in any operational

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.4.4: All Vehicle Controls must stay below the top-most point of the Front Hoop in any operational
T.1.4.4 All Vehicle Controls must stay below the top-most point of the Front Hoop in any operational 
position

RULE: t.9.4.3
            TITLE: inertia switch operation:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.9.4.3: Inertia Switch operation:
T.9.4.3 Inertia Switch operation: 
a. Must trigger due to a longitudinal impact load which decelerates the vehicle at between 
8 g and 11 g depending on the duration of the deceleration (refer to spec sheet of the 
Sensata device) 
b. Must Open the Shutdown Circuit IC.9.2.2 / EV.7.2.2 if triggered 
c. Must latch until manually reset  
d. May be reset by the driver from inside the driver's cell
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 74 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
VE - VEHICLE AND DRIVER EQUIPMENT

RULE: d.3.10.1
            TITLE: if a vehicle stalls and cannot restart without external assistance, or is damaged and not able to

            CONTENT:
            rule D.3.10.1: If a vehicle stalls and cannot restart without external assistance, or is damaged and not able to
D.3.10.1 If a vehicle stalls and cannot restart without external assistance, or is damaged and not able to 
complete the run, it will be scored DNF for that run

RULE: in.12.3.3
            TITLE: the tractive system active light may switch a short time after the vehicle has come to a

            CONTENT:
            rule IN.12.3.3: The Tractive System Active Light may switch a short time after the vehicle has come to a
IN.12.3.3 The Tractive System Active Light may switch a short time after the vehicle has come to a 
complete stop as the reduction of the system voltage is not immediate.  See EV.7.2.2.c

RULE: f.11.2.2
            TITLE: rear impact protection

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.2.2: Rear Impact Protection
F.11.2.2 Rear Impact Protection 
a. All Accumulator Containers must be protected from rear impact:  
• When the rear impact structure is 100 mm or less from an Accumulator Container, 
the structure must be Equivalent to Side Impact Structure (F.6.4, F.7.5)  
• When the rear impact structure is more than 100 mm from an Accumulator 
Container, the structure must meet  F.5.16 Component Protection 
• The Accumulator Container must not be part of the Equivalent structure. 
b. The Rear Impact Protection for the Accumulator Container must: 
• Go to the Upper Side Impact Height at minimum
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 53 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
• Have a structural and triangulated load path from the top of the Rear Impact 
Protection to the Upper Side Impact Structure  F.6.4, F.7.5 at the Main Hoop  
• Have a structural and triangulated load path from the bottom of the Rear Impact 
Protection to the Lower Side Impact Structure  F.6.4, F.7.5 at the Main Hoop  
c. Other Tractive System components in a position below 350 mm from the ground must be 
protected from rear impact by structure meeting F.5.16 Component Protection

RULE: t.1.8.4
            TITLE: details

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.8.4: Details
T.1.8.4 Details 
a. Firewalls composed of multiple panels must overlap and be sealed at the joints 
Sealing between firewalls must not be a stressed part of the Firewall 
b. Grommets must be used to seal any pass through for wiring, cables, etc
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 58 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
c. Any seals or adhesives used with the Firewall must be rated for the application 
environment

RULE: in.8.2.3
            TITLE: if any vehicle on track is observed to have large, uncontrolled movements of aerodynamic

            CONTENT:
            rule IN.8.2.3: If any vehicle on track is observed to have large, uncontrolled movements of Aerodynamic
IN.8.2.3 If any vehicle on track is observed to have large, uncontrolled movements of Aerodynamic 
Devices, then officials may Black Flag the vehicle for IN.15 Reinspection.

RULE: ic.3.3.3
            TITLE: failure of any component of the throttle system must not prevent the throttle returning to

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.3.3.3: Failure of any component of the throttle system must not prevent the throttle returning to
IC.3.3.3 Failure of any component of the throttle system must not prevent the throttle returning to 
the closed position.

RULE: f.11.2.3
            TITLE: all non crushable items should have a minimum 25 mm clearance to the surface of the

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.2.3: All Non Crushable Items should have a minimum 25 mm clearance to the surface of the
F.11.2.3 All Non Crushable Items should have a minimum 25 mm clearance to the surface of the 
Accumulator Container. 
Non Crushable Items include, but are not limited to motors, differentials, and the side or rear 
impact structure itself.  Accumulator mounts do not require clearance

RULE: f.11.2.4
            TITLE: non crushable items mounted behind the rear impact structure must not be able to come

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.2.4: Non Crushable Items mounted behind the Rear Impact structure must not be able to come
F.11.2.4 Non Crushable Items mounted behind the Rear Impact structure must not be able to come 
through the Rear Impact structure.
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 54 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
T - TECHNICAL ASPECTS
